title: Add kittui-md tables-json inspection mode
description: Add --tables-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for parsed Markdown tables, emitting schema_version plus indexed table rows, alignments, column widths, and footprint records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add bracketed-paste aware native paste
description:
Large terminal automation gap: kittwm has exact byte injection, but no native paste primitive that respects bracketed-paste mode. Modern shells/editors enable DEC private `?2004` so pasted multi-line input should be wrapped in `ESC[200~` / `ESC[201~`; otherwise automation can accidentally execute partial lines or break editor semantics.

Scope:
- Track bracketed-paste mode (`CSI ? 2004 h/l`) in native PTY `TerminalState`.
- Publish optional `bracketed_paste` status metadata in native pane status surfaces.
- Add socket command `PASTE_BYTES_B64 <window|focused> <base64>` that wraps bytes in bracketed-paste markers when the target pane currently has bracketed paste enabled, otherwise sends raw bytes.
- Add CLI wrapper `kittwm --paste-file WINDOW PATH|-`.
- Update HELP/docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves bracketed-paste mode tracking.
- Daemon/session-level tests prove status metadata and queued paste command.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md metadata-blocks-json inspection mode
description: Add --metadata-blocks-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown YAML/pluses metadata blocks, emitting schema_version plus indexed kind/source records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md renders Markdown lists as bullet and numbered components
description: Add Markdown list awareness to the kittui-affordances renderer so unordered and ordered lists preserve bullet/number markers instead of flattening items into plain paragraphs. Include tests for bullet and ordered list output and keep link chips/paragraph rendering behavior intact.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: add delete command for image and placement cleanup
description:
Large gap: shell workflows can now render and re-place kitty image ids, but there is no `kittui` CLI command to delete an image or a specific placement from the terminal. Scripts must know the low-level kitty delete escape grammar.

Scope:
- Add `kittui delete --id ID [--placement-id P]` to emit kitty delete escapes.
- Support decimal and `0x...` image id parsing (reuse existing parser).
- Honor `--json`/`--json-bytes` by reporting delete byte counts and optional delete string.
- Add CLI tests for image delete and placement delete.

Acceptance:
- `kittui delete --id 0x1234 --json --json-bytes` emits machine-readable delete bytes.
- `kittui delete --id 0x1234 --placement-id 7` uses placement delete form.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm v2: backend-agnostic compositor (local/remote/Mac/X11 windows as homogeneous kittui chrome)
description:
Generalise kittui-wm so any window — local X11, remote X11 over SSH, native macOS via Quartz capture, XQuartz-hosted X11, future Wayland, future Tendril — appears in the user's terminal as the same kittui chrome surface. The compositor multiplexes multiple XServer backends behind one operator UI, with globally-unique window ids, per-window WindowMode (Floating or Tiled), per-window chrome theme, and a single hit-test/route_pointer/route_key pipeline. v2 must let the operator mix Mac windows (Quartz CGWindowList capture) with XQuartz X11 windows in the same session, all rendered with locally-composed kittui chrome.

2026-05-22 burn-down note: implementation child beads for native PTY default, native browser, socket env/protocol, XQuartz wrapper, picker overlay, relative placement, animation grammar, docs, and markdown/UI initial stack landed and were closed. Remaining SSH latency proof is linked as dependent/future work rather than blocking this implementation burn-down.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native PTY close-pane lifecycle
description:
Large gap: the native terminal WM path can split/focus panes, but there is no WM-level close-pane action. Users must exit shells manually and the pane model does not reclaim dead/focused panes.

Scope:
- Add `PtyTerminalApp::terminate()` using the portable-pty child kill hook.
- Add a native-session close-focused-pane chord (e.g. `Ctrl-A x`) that terminates and removes the focused pane when more than one pane exists.
- Reflow remaining panes and choose a sane next focus after close.
- Poll for exited PTY children and remove dead panes automatically when more than one pane remains.
- Document the keybinding.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover focus selection after removing panes.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
- README/docs list split/focus/close native pane controls.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-affordances: add first-party form and control components
description:
Renderer/component gap: kittui-affordances has reusable chrome and some components, but lacks a first-party form/control palette needed by semantic kittwm surfaces and SDK apps: buttons, checkboxes, radio buttons/groups, text inputs/areas, selects, sliders, progress bars, menus, tabs, split panes, lists, and tables with focus/disabled/selected states.

Scope:
- Add first-party primitive-scene builders in kittui-affordances for a minimal form/control set, keeping kittui core primitive-only.
- Expose JSON/CLI-friendly component scene helpers where appropriate.
- Include state variants (focused, selected/checked, disabled, active) and theme hooks compatible with kittwm chrome.
- Add tests/examples proving the controls produce primitive kittui scenes.

Acceptance:
- At least button, checkbox, radio/radio-group, text-input, select/list, progress, tabs, and split-pane affordance builders exist.
- Components are in kittui-affordances (or a similarly high-level crate), not kittui-core.
- kittwm/SDK can depend on these without duplicating renderer logic.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md renders Markdown task-list checkboxes
description: Enable pulldown-cmark task-list parsing and render unchecked/checked task list markers in kittui-md output, preserving list bullets and adding tests for [ ] and [x] items.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md images-only mode for image reference inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --images mode that prints only parsed image references with alt text and URLs. This complements --references and --metadata-json for focused image audits. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm native app: headless browser backend with screenshot + mouse/keyboard control
description: Operator direction: support local headless browser apps regardless of X/Quartz backend. Implement a kittui-wm native app backend that launches/attaches to a headless Chromium/Chrome instance, captures screenshots into kittui frames, and routes mouse + keyboard events back to the page. Acceptance: can open a URL/data page, render screenshots in kittwm, click/type through kittui input, and update captures without depending on XQuartz/xterm. Parent epic: bd-031e54. Filed from bd-a9ec5b session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Layout helper for virtual-to-relative kitty placement anchors
description: Build a reusable layout helper for kitty virtual placements anchored to relative non-virtual placements. It should wrap PlacementOptions::relative and expose a higher-level API for: create virtual anchor, place cell/grid images relative to it with H/V offsets, set z ordering, and reserve/update terminal cell footprints. This is needed by markdown tables and future rich UI components that need background images below connected cell graphics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md renders Markdown definition lists
description: Enable pulldown-cmark definition-list parsing and render definition list title/definition pairs as visible components instead of dropping structure. Add tests for a simple term/definition pair.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Exercise metadata blocks in kittui-md proof gallery
description: Update docs/examples/kittui-md-proof.md so the proof document includes Markdown metadata/frontmatter blocks now supported by kittui-md. Keep it small and validate with metadata-blocks/metadata-json modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: include base64 PNGs in render batch JSON bytes
description:
Large gap: `kittui render` single-scene JSON supports `--json-bytes` via `png_base64`, and FFI render-many manifests include base64 PNG entries, but CLI batch render manifests omit PNG bytes even with global `--json-bytes`. This leaves shell users without a no-files JSON transport for batch render previews.

Scope:
- Extend `kittui render <scenes.json>|- --out-dir DIR --json-bytes --json/--dry-run` to include per-file `png_base64` entries.
- Keep default batch JSON compact without base64.
- Add integration test for batch dry-run JSON bytes.

Acceptance:
- Batch render dry-run with `--json-bytes` includes valid PNG base64 per image.
- Existing render_command tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md exposes structured definition-list metadata
description: Definition lists now render visibly, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose term/definition pairs. Add structured definition list metadata, include it in metadata JSON and plain/rich metadata sections, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm launcher preview: boxed searchable launcher menu
description: Add a visible 'kitwm launcher [--filter QUERY] [--limit N]' preview command that renders a boxed, numbered launcher menu using the same PATH/macOS candidate source as 'kitwm apps'. This is the first operator-visible floating-launcher-shaped UI surface before wiring the overlay into the live raw-mode session. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add real native PTY split panes
description:
Large gap: no-arg `kittwm` is still a single fullscreen PTY proof, not a terminal WM. It lacks a pane list, focus model, real split action, and per-pane geometry.

Scope:
- Replace the single `PtyTerminalApp` in the native loop with a small native pane model.
- Implement one high-value split keybinding/action that spawns a second shell PTY and lays panes out 50/50.
- Route keyboard input only to the focused pane and support focus cycling between native panes.
- Render minimal per-pane title/focus chrome and recompute pane sizes on terminal resize.

Acceptance:
- No-arg `kittwm` starts with one PTY.
- The split binding creates a second visible PTY pane.
- Focus can move between panes and typing goes only to the focused pane.
- Resize updates pane PTY dimensions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: preserve basic SGR colors in native PTY renderer
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap: native PTY panes currently discard SGR styling and render every glyph in one cyan-on-dark palette. Many TUIs and shells rely on ANSI color and reverse video for legibility, so native kittwm should preserve basic cell style in its renderer while keeping text snapshots plain text.

Scope:
- Store per-cell foreground/background colors and current SGR style in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle common SGR modes: reset, bold intensity, foreground/background 30-37/90-97 and 40-47/100-107, default fg/bg, and reverse video.
- Render cell backgrounds and pseudo-glyph foreground colors accordingly.
- Preserve existing plain text snapshots, scrollback, alternate-screen, resize/edit behavior.
- Add focused parser/render tests.

Acceptance:
- Parser tests show SGR style applies to written cells and resets.
- Render test proves colored foreground/background affects RGBA output.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: introduce reusable kittui chrome theme adapter
description:
Large gap: kittwm chrome is hard-coded in compositor/session paths instead of being reusable kittui UI/chrome. This blocks themed WM chrome, scriptable previews, and shared chrome between live WM and tools.

Scope:
- Introduce a reusable kittwm window chrome/theme adapter based on kittui scenes/layers.
- Model focused/unfocused state, title/source label, tiled/floating hints, border/glow/shadow palette.
- Replace hard-coded compositor border colors/shape with default theme data.
- Document how a host can use/override the default chrome theme.

Acceptance:
- Fake backend demo renders distinct focused vs unfocused chrome.
- Unit tests assert layer ordering and state-dependent colors.
- No compositor hot-path fixed chrome literals remain outside default theme definitions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make native session restore transactional
description:
Large native-WM reliability gap: `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON` currently terminates and clears all existing panes before spawning replacement panes. If a restore pane fails to spawn, the live session can be left empty or the native loop can error out. Session restore should be transactional from the user's perspective.

Scope:
- Spawn all restore panes into a temporary vector first.
- Only after all new panes spawn and resize successfully, terminate and replace old panes.
- Preserve the old session and log an error if restore spawning/resizing fails.
- Keep title/weight/focus/layout restore behavior unchanged on success.
- Add targeted helper/unit coverage where practical.

Acceptance:
- Restore no longer clears current panes before new pane creation succeeds.
- Targeted session tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --launch-on-f12: runtime launcher key spawns xterm/command
description: Small runtime launcher slice under bd-031e54 / bd-8d64b1. Add --launch-on-f12 so a running kitwm session intercepts F12 before forwarding keys and spawns xterm by default, or  via '/bin/sh -c'. The spawn is detached with stdio null, logged to /tmp/kittui-wm.log, and shown in the footer as last_launch_pid. This proves in-session launch plumbing before Mod+Return + floating menu. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document CLI render-only PNG command
description:
Large gap: `kittui render` now exists as the CLI render-only PNG surface, but README/DESIGN still only advertise compose/place flows. Shell users looking for artifact/preview rendering will miss the new command.

Scope:
- Update README shipped CLI command list and quick start with `kittui render` examples.
- Update DESIGN FFI/platform or CLI section to mention the render-only PNG path across Rust/FFI/Python/CLI.
- Keep docs concise and accurate.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention `kittui render <scene.json|-> --out out.png` and render-only PNG use case.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md outline-json inspection mode
description: Add --outline-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown heading outline entries, emitting schema_version plus indexed level/text/anchor records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add capability scoping for SDK clients
description: Define and enforce client capabilities for create/replace windows, spawn/capture surfaces, send input, clipboard read/write, and global event subscriptions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive tables toggle
description: Add a native tables toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press t to inspect parsed table summaries without leaving the pager. Reuse existing table metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/table-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: extract presentation-agnostic shell view and chrome model
description: Split the default shell's layout/chrome view model from direct ANSI/kitty output so it can be rendered by kitty graphics, pure terminal, kittui scene, or headless renderers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: extract TerminalSurface engine from PtyTerminalApp
description: Move PTY lifecycle, TerminalState parsing, responses, host sequences, snapshots, and RGBA rendering behind a reusable TerminalSurface type. Keep PtyTerminalApp as a thin adapter with no behavior change.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: type channelized batch placement output
description:
Large gap: `Kittui.placeManyChannels` returns a structured object at runtime, but TypeScript declarations type it as `Record<string, unknown>`. Platform users lose compile-time access to image ids, footprints, byte counts, and channel strings.

Scope:
- Add a `BatchChannels` TypeScript interface matching FFI/CLI channelized batch JSON.
- Update `placeManyChannels` declaration to return `BatchChannels`.
- Add a lightweight type/runtime test expectation in the existing JS tests where practical and README wording.

Acceptance:
- Type declarations expose count, image_ids, footprints, upload_bytes, placement_bytes, embed_bytes, upload, placement, embed.
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: capture OSC titles for native pane chrome
description:
Large terminal-WM chrome gap: native PTY pane titles are frozen to the launch command unless manually renamed. Real terminal apps set their window title through OSC 0/1/2; kittwm should consume that metadata so WM chrome/status follows the running app.

Scope:
- Teach the native PTY terminal parser to capture OSC 0/1/2 title updates.
- Make `PtyTerminalApp::title()` return the captured terminal title when available, falling back to launch command.
- Preserve explicit socket `RENAME_PANE` override semantics in session chrome/status.
- Add targeted parser/unit coverage.

Acceptance:
- OSC title sequences update native app title.
- Existing explicit display title override still wins at session level.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md capabilities discovery outputs
description: Add --capabilities and --capabilities-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can list supported high-level capability names independently of --about. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: add real JSON runtime config constructor
description:
Large gap: `kittui-ffi` exposes only `kittui_runtime_new(cache_dir)` and a placeholder `kittui_runtime_configure` that records config text without mutating runtime state. Non-Rust platforms need a real constructor for renderer/transport/terminal capability config.

Scope:
- Add `kittui_runtime_new_config(const char* json)` that constructs a runtime from JSON.
- Support at least: `cache_dir`, `renderer`, `transport`, `columns`, `rows`, `cell_width_px`, `cell_height_px`, `supports_kitty`, and `supports_unicode_placeholders`.
- Keep existing `kittui_runtime_new` behavior.
- Update ABI minor and add targeted FFI tests for transport/config and unsupported-terminal errors.

Acceptance:
- FFI tests can create a direct/supported runtime and place a scene.
- FFI tests can create an unsupported runtime and observe placement failure/last_error.
- `cargo test -p kittui-ffi` and `cargo build -p kittui-ffi` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm runtime toggles: Ctrl-A f fullscreen and Ctrl-A t floating state
description: Port two safe defaults from ~/collective/modules/home-manager/wm.nix into the terminal-safe Ctrl-A keymap: Mod+f -> Ctrl-A f fullscreen toggle, Mod+t -> Ctrl-A t float toggle. Add actions to the keymap vocabulary, default bindings, live runtime state, footer/log visibility, and tests. Parent: bd-031e54; supports configurable WM defaults.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md preserves fenced code language labels
description: Render fenced code block language info in Markdown output so blocks like ```rust carry a visible code:rust label in the generated kittui component. Add tests for fenced and unlabeled code blocks while preserving existing code block textbox behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: include FFI last_error in thrown failures
description:
Large gap: TypeScript bindings throw only numeric status codes when FFI placement calls fail, even though the C ABI exposes `kittui_last_error`. Python now reports last-error details, but JS/TS platform users still lose the actual parse/runtime message.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_last_error` in the TypeScript koffi binding.
- Add a helper that appends last-error text to all non-OK placement errors.
- Cover place/placeAt/placeMany/placeManyAt/placeManyChannels failure paths with fake-lib tests.
- Update TypeScript README to mention detailed FFI errors.

Acceptance:
- Failed FFI calls throw messages containing both status and last error text when available.
- Existing TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Set q=2 quiet flag on all kitty control commands
description:
kitty emits diagnostic responses for upload, animation, placement, and delete commands unless q=1 (suppress OK responses) or q=2 (suppress all responses including errors) is set. The current encoder never sets q, which leaks Gi=<id>;ENOENT and OK lines into the terminal output and breaks the showcase visually. Wire a per-Runtime quiet mode (default q=2) and surface it via TerminalInfo / Runtime builder; preserve a verbose path for debugging.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md metadata-blocks inspection mode
description: Add a focused --metadata-blocks output mode to kittui-md so frontmatter/metadata blocks have the same human inspection affordance as links, images, tables, code blocks, definitions, math, HTML, and footnotes. Include CLI docs and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md toc alias for outline
description: Add --toc as a friendly alias for kittui-md --outline so users can inspect a document table of contents with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native app discovery CLI wrappers
description:
Large native-WM socket usability gap: docs and controllers still use raw `kittwm --attach -c APPS_JSON` / `APPS_FIRST ...` for app discovery, even though other inspection/control/automation paths now have first-class wrappers. App discovery and launch are part of the DISPLAY-like WM control plane and should be scriptable without raw protocol strings.

Scope:
- Add CLI flags:
  - `--apps-json` -> `APPS_JSON`
  - `--apps-first <query>` -> `APPS_FIRST <query>`
  - `--apps-launch-first <query>` -> `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST <query>`
- Preserve query case/spaces.
- Add help text, request-builder tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Wrappers generate expected socket requests.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui: add render_many_png batch API
description:
Large gap: kittui has batch placement APIs but render-only PNG is single-scene only. CLI batch rendering currently loops locally, and Rust hosts that need preview/artifact batches have to do the same boilerplate instead of using a first-class renderer API.

Scope:
- Add `Runtime::render_many_png(&[Scene]) -> Vec<Vec<u8>>` preserving input order.
- Use it in `kittui render --out-dir` batch path.
- Add core tests for order/count/PNG signatures and empty batches.
- Keep terminal placement support checks out of render-only APIs.

Acceptance:
- Core batch render API returns one PNG per scene and accepts empty batches.
- CLI render batch tests continue to pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make pointer hit-testing deterministic by z-order
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm pointer routing says it walks windows in z-order, but `hit_test` iterates a `HashMap`, so overlapping windows route nondeterministically. A terminal WM needs deterministic top-window pointer focus before swap/raise/lower features can be meaningful.

Scope:
- Track last composition/raw-frame window order alongside placement rectangles.
- Make `hit_test` walk that order from topmost to bottommost instead of iterating the placement HashMap.
- Preserve pointer coordinate mapping behavior.
- Add targeted tests for overlapping windows proving the later/top window wins.

Acceptance:
- Overlapping placements route pointer events to the deterministic topmost window.
- Both raw-frame and scene composition paths update hit-test order.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md images-json inspection mode
description: Add --images-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for parsed Markdown image references, emitting schema_version plus indexed alt/url/title records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: absolute placement ignores footprint.x/y so images render at cursor not requested coords
description: Runtime::place emits placement_command(image_id, scene.footprint, transport) which uses footprint.cols/rows but drops footprint.x/y. Kitty graphics protocol has no absolute positioning verb — caller must move the cursor via CSI H first. We never do, so a scene at (col=10,row=5) lands wherever the cursor happens to be. Need to prepend the placement with \x1b[r;cH when footprint.x/y are non-zero (or expose a separate api). Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: expose compose placement overrides
description:
Large gap: kittui now has `Runtime::place_at` so hosts can render scene-local assets and move them without changing scene ids, but the CLI `compose` path still places scenes only at their embedded footprint. Shell scripts must mutate JSON coordinates to move a composed scene.

Scope:
- Add optional `kittui compose <scene.json>|- --x X --y Y` placement overrides.
- Use `Runtime::place_at` so render/cache identity remains scene-local while terminal placement changes.
- Preserve existing compose behavior when no overrides are supplied.
- Add CLI/integration coverage that pipes `--scene-json` into `compose - --x ... --y ... --dry-run --json`.

Acceptance:
- `kittui box --scene-json | kittui compose - --x 5 --y 6 --dry-run --json` reports footprint x=5 y=6 with original cols/rows.
- Existing `compose -` behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document native pane process metadata
description:
Gap: native kittwm socket status now publishes per-pane `pid` and `command`, but README/docs still describe only weight and geometry. Document the fields so controller authors can depend on them.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md native status paragraph to include optional `pid` and `command` fields in PANES_JSON/STATUS_JSON.
- Update README socket control example comment to mention process metadata.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention `pid` and `command` for native pane status.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm apps --json: machine-readable launcher candidate list
description: Add --json support to 'kitwm apps' so future floating launcher UI / daemon attach can consume the candidate source programmatically. Output should include default_command, default_resolved, path_commands[], macos_apps[] where applicable. Parent: bd-031e54; extends bd-7e7f3d / decomposes bd-8d64b1.

title: Add categories to kittui-md schemas-json
description: Extend --schemas-json so each schema entry includes the mode category, aligning schema discovery with the rest of the mode discovery JSON surfaces. Document it and add output tests.

title: kittui-md rich render mode: emit actual kitty graphics components
description: Next proposal: kittui-md currently emits a textual/component proof. Add rich render mode that turns MarkdownDocument/UiComponent into actual kittui scenes and kitty graphics placements in a normal terminal, with scrolling and resize handling. Builds on bd-f81b60 initial stack.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md math-only mode for expression inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --math mode that prints only parsed inline/display math expressions with kind and source. This complements --code-blocks and --definitions for focused extraction. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Move kittui_wm_demo example to use kittui_cli::session::run_loop
description: The session loop has been extracted into kittui_cli::session for the new kitwm binary (bd-c60dd8). The kittui_wm_demo example still has its own copy. Replace the example's run_loop with a call to kittui_cli::session::run_loop and delete the duplicated raw-mode + debugger code from the example. Reduces example to ~80 lines of backend setup.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: implement more native PTY cursor CSI modes
description:
Terminal fidelity gap: native PTY cursor movement only handles A/B/C/D and H/f. Common terminal programs also use cursor-horizontal-absolute (`CSI n G`), vertical-position-absolute (`CSI n d`), horizontal/vertical relative forms (`CSI n a/e`), and next/previous line (`CSI n E/F`). Missing these sequences can corrupt rendered panes and READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Implement CSI `G`, `d`, `a`, `e`, `E`, and `F` in native `TerminalState`.
- Preserve existing 1-based/clamped semantics.
- Add focused parser/text snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Cursor absolute/relative movement modes update output positions correctly.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add primitive gradient scene helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: Python/TypeScript scene helpers now make solid boxes easy, but gradient scenes still require hand-authored Scene JSON (and the existing TS backgroundLinear helper is only a placeholder layer). Gradients are one of kittui's core shell-renderer primitives and should be first-party in platform bindings.

Scope:
- Add Python `scene.gradient_layer(...)` and `scene.gradient_box(...)` helpers.
- Add TypeScript `scene.gradientLayer(...)` and `scene.gradientBox(...)` helpers.
- Keep helpers primitive-only and JSON-compatible.
- Add binding tests and README examples.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS users can build a valid gradient Scene without hand-authoring schema.
- Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md examples discovery outputs
description: Add --examples and --examples-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can list common invocation examples. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm config inspect + Ctrl-A Shift-r reload config state
description: Port the safe collective WM reloadConfig default into terminal-safe kitwm. Add action reload.config, default bind Ctrl-A Shift-r, live runtime reload counter/log/footer state, and a visible 'kitwm config' command that prints config paths/env (KITTUI_WM_KEYMAP, KITTUI_WM_LAUNCH_CMD, KITTUI_WM_LAUNCH_QUERY) plus keymap validation status. Parent: bd-031e54; supports configurable WM defaults.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive definitions toggle
description: Add a native definitions toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press d to inspect definition-list entries without leaving the pager. Reuse existing definition metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/definition-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --serve binds Unix socket + --attach connects (minimal protocol)
description: Minimal daemon/client v1 for bd-fb5d9d/bd-aaea73. kitwm --serve binds $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (default /tmp/kitwm-0.sock) and accepts one client at a time. kitwm --attach connects, sends a Hello, receives a Welcome, runs a passthrough where stdin from the client goes to the daemon and the daemon's render bytes come back. Backend hosted by the daemon; client is a renderer only. Parent: bd-fb5d9d/bd-aaea73.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm live launcher overlay: Ctrl-A Enter opens type-to-filter boxed menu
description: Move the launcher preview into the live raw-mode session. Add --launcher-overlay so Ctrl-A Enter opens a boxed overlay in-session; typed chars filter candidates, Backspace edits, Enter launches selected first row, Esc closes. Keep default behavior without the flag for compatibility. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md metadata JSON records input path when available
description: Extend kittui-md --metadata-json source metadata to include the input file path when a file is provided, while keeping stdin output path null. Add tests for both path and stdin-style cases.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: SSH operator walkthrough
description:
docs/operator-walkthrough.md: step-by-step for the SSH-into-Mac use case. Includes generating an SSH key for kittui-wm, enabling Screen Recording for sshd, setting KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY, attaching, detaching, killing the session. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: kittwm: expose dirty-frame metrics in native status
description:
Follow-up from bd-510a36. Expose dirty-frame metrics so renderer policy and debugging can observe how much of each native surface changes over time.

Scope:
- Track dirty tile count/fraction for native raw-frame surfaces when dirty-grid measurement is enabled.
- Expose the latest dirty metrics in native status/events or a narrow debug surface.
- Avoid changing rendering behavior.
- Add tests for metric calculation and serialization/status exposure.

Acceptance:
- Dirty tile count/fraction can be observed without enabling partial updates.
- No default rendering behavior changes.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive reload status messages
description: Add an in-pager status message for kittui-md --interactive reloads so pressing r reports reload success or failure without silently changing state or tearing down the pager on transient file errors. Document behavior and add targeted tests for status rendering/reload handling.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive images toggle
description: Add a native images toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press i to inspect document image references without leaving the pager. Reuse existing image metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/image-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: deliberately slow backend cannot stall UI thread
description: Add a SlowFakeServer that sleeps 500ms in capture(). Attach two backends to a MultiCompositor: SlowFakeServer + FakeServer. Assert that tick() returns within 33ms regardless of the slow backend's state, and that the slow backend's window eventually shows a non-empty frame after its first capture completes. This is the regression test for the pump architecture. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add pure terminal renderer for shell view model
description: Implement an old-terminal/DEC/Unicode fallback renderer for the shell view model that draws panes/chrome as terminal text rather than kitty graphics frames.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: make runtime_configure mutate runtime
description:
Large gap: `kittui_runtime_configure` returns Ok but only stores the requested JSON in `last_error`; it does not actually change runtime configuration. Platform bindings and long-lived hosts need a real reconfigure path rather than destroying/reopening handles.

Scope:
- Implement `kittui_runtime_configure(runtime, json)` by rebuilding `rt.inner` from the same JSON config shape as `kittui_runtime_new_config`.
- Clear `last_error` on success; set it and return a non-OK status on bad JSON/config/build errors.
- Add targeted FFI tests proving transport/probe and terminal support change after configure.

Acceptance:
- Configure changes probe transport/renderer-visible config on an existing runtime.
- Configure can disable terminal placement support and subsequent place returns Runtime error.
- Targeted FFI tests and ABI snapshot still pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm: nix package should bundle quartz backend on macOS
description: Operator report: when installed as nix package, 'kitwm --backend quartz' fails with 'Quartz backend requires --features quartz on macOS'. The flake builds kittui-cli with default features (none). Fix: pass cargoBuildFlags/buildFeatures so darwin builds get sck (and therefore quartz) and linux builds get xvfb, so the system-native backend is always available without rebuild flags. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm runtime focus actions: Ctrl-A C-hjkl visible focus direction state
description: Wire keymap focus actions into live kitwm runtime state. Ctrl-A C-h/j/k/l should update a visible focus direction marker in the footer and log focus.left/down/up/right transitions. This makes another configurable action family real while tiling/window membership wiring catches up. Parent: bd-031e54; follows bd-5edc97/bd-7f00ef.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket help catalog
description:
Large gap: the live native kittwm session socket has grown many commands (SPAWN_PTY, STATUS_JSON, PANES_JSON, FOCUS_PANE, CLOSE_PANE, LAYOUT, APPS*), but unlike the standalone daemon it has no HELP surface. Script authors have to know command names out of band.

Scope:
- Add `HELP` / `?` to `NativeSpawnQueue`.
- Add `HELP_JSON` with a machine-readable command catalog, categories, and short descriptions.
- Include all current native socket commands.
- Add targeted tests and update docs/wm.md.

Acceptance:
- `HELP` lists SPAWN_PTY, STATUS_JSON, PANES_JSON, FOCUS_PANE, CLOSE_PANE, LAYOUT, and APPS_JSON.
- `HELP_JSON` parses and contains a command record for LAYOUT.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: expose drag events in SEND_MOUSE
description:
Follow-up gap after native mouse drag routing: host mouse routing now preserves drag motion internally, but the public `SEND_MOUSE` socket/CLI command still only accepts generic `move`. External controllers cannot inject drag motions (`move-left`, `move-middle`, `move-right`) even though the session encoder supports them.

Scope:
- Extend `SEND_MOUSE` validation and `kittwm --send-mouse` validation to accept `move-left`, `move-middle`, and `move-right`.
- Update HELP/docs/README examples or wording for accepted events.
- Add focused daemon/CLI tests.

Acceptance:
- `SEND_MOUSE focused move-left 7 9` queues successfully.
- CLI helper accepts `move-left` and rejects unknown events.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm runtime layout actions: Ctrl-A e toggle split and Ctrl-A = balance
description: Port two more safe defaults from ~/collective/modules/home-manager/wm.nix into terminal-safe Ctrl-A keymap: Mod+e toggleSplit -> Ctrl-A e toggle split axis, Mod+= balanceWindows -> Ctrl-A = balance. Add keymap vocabulary/defaults, visible runtime layout state in footer/log, and tests. Parent: bd-031e54; supports configurable WM defaults.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: write render metadata manifest files
description:
Large shell-renderer gap: `kittui render <scenes.json> --out-dir DIR` writes deterministic PNG files, but the manifest is only available via stdout JSON modes. Shell scripts and external CI artifact workflows often need the manifest persisted alongside the files without redirecting/parsing stdout.

Scope:
- Add `kittui render ... --manifest PATH` for render metadata output.
- Support single-scene and batch render paths.
- For batch `--out-dir`, allow `--manifest manifest.json` to write the same manifest that `--json` would print, including filenames and optional base64 under `--json-bytes`.
- For single `--out`, write single render metadata to the manifest path.
- Add tests and README docs.

Acceptance:
- `kittui render scenes.json --out-dir DIR --manifest DIR/manifest.json` writes PNGs and a manifest file without requiring `--json`.
- Targeted render command tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kitty spec deviations and conformance status
description:
Add a docs/protocol-conformance.md (or DESIGN.md section) that enumerates every section of https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ and marks kittui's status as supported / partial / unsupported, with links to the bead tracking any partial/unsupported items. Update README.md status line once the P0/P1 children of this epic land.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md components-only mode for generated component inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --components mode that prints only generated component records, without outline/link/image/metadata sections. This gives a concise human-readable view of the Markdown-to-component conversion. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

description:
Practical demo + script: launch kittui-wm serve on host A, ssh from host B with KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:0 forwarded, run kittui-wm attach. Confirm Quartz capture pixels travel over SSH and pointer events route back. Capture screenshots from both terminals to prove same-session-different-viewers. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: kittwm: add native socket layout axis command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes support row/column layouts from keyboard shortcuts, and the socket can spawn/focus/close panes, but external controllers cannot switch the live layout axis. A DISPLAY-like terminal WM needs scriptable layout control.

Scope:
- Add a typed native socket command `LAYOUT columns|rows`.
- Include the current layout axis in native socket `STATUS` output.
- Apply layout commands in the native session loop, reflowing visible panes and updating status snapshots.
- Add queue parsing and layout-axis helper tests.
- Document the attach command in docs/wm.md.

Acceptance:
- `LAYOUT rows` over the native session socket queues a layout command.
- `STATUS` can report `layout=rows` or `layout=columns`.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Make the wgpu backend a production-quality library backend
description: Operator direction: use the cacophony project as inspiration for canonical wgpu setup examples, but do not cargo-cult it because GPU support there may not have worked reliably. Treat kittui as a well-written library: design a robust wgpu backend abstraction with explicit adapter/device lifecycle, headless/offscreen compatibility on macOS/Linux, deterministic CPU fallback, clear errors, resource reuse across frames, no hidden global state, parity tests against CPU for supported nodes, and documentation of unsupported GPU features. This should refine/coordinate with bd-6c1165 (probe cache) and the renderer semantic beads rather than leaving GPU as a best-effort stub.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: make probe report detected terminal capabilities
description:
Large gap: `kittui probe` currently reports hard-coded kitty support instead of the actual `TerminalInfo::detect()` result, making it unreliable for shell scripts and external hosts that need renderer/transport decisions.

Scope:
- Update `kittui probe` to emit the detected terminal descriptor (`columns`, `rows`, `cell_size`, `supports_kitty`, `supports_unicode_placeholders`, `transport`) plus renderer/version/config provenance.
- Preserve `--force` cache invalidation semantics.
- Add targeted tests for JSON shape using environment-controlled detection, especially tmux passthrough and Windows Terminal unsupported kitty cases.

Acceptance:
- `kittui probe --json` no longer hard-codes capability booleans.
- Tests assert probe output follows `TerminalInfo::detect()`.
- Existing CLI build remains green.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md metadata JSON includes component details
description: Extend kittui-md --metadata-json to include a stable components_detail array with component kind, text, and suggested width/height. Keep the existing components count for compatibility and add tests for the new JSON shape.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: implement native PTY edit CSI modes
description:
Terminal fidelity gap: native PTY snapshots do not handle common edit CSI sequences used by line editors and TUIs: insert/delete characters, erase characters, and insert/delete lines. Missing these sequences leaves stale characters in kittwm panes and `READ_TEXT` automation output.

Scope:
- Implement CSI `@` (insert character), `P` (delete character), `X` (erase character), `L` (insert line), and `M` (delete line) in native `TerminalState`.
- Clamp counts and operate within the current row / screen.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Text snapshot tests demonstrate each edit mode.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: expose runtime configure
description:
Large gap: FFI and Python now support live runtime reconfiguration, but TypeScript hosts still must close/reopen to change renderer/transport/terminal options.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_runtime_configure` in TypeScript koffi binding.
- Add `Kittui.configure(options)` using the same option normalization as constructor and returning `this`.
- Add type declaration and fake-lib tests for success/failure last_error detail.
- Update TypeScript README example/API text.

Acceptance:
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` passes.
- configure forwards normalized JSON and errors include FFI last_error.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md components mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --components mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal: add standalone first-party terminal app skeleton
description: Add a standalone kittwm-terminal/kittui-term binary that connects through kittwm-sdk, creates/replaces a window, and hosts a TerminalSurface with initial config/profile hooks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md version discovery outputs
description: Add --version and --version-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query the binary/package version independently of --about. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement Transport::File and Transport::Memory upload paths
description:
Transport enum currently advertises File and Memory but encode_chunked always emits a=t,f=100 streaming base64. Per spec t= selects the transmission medium (d direct, f file, t temp file, s shared memory) and the payload becomes a path or shm name instead of pixel bytes. Implement real File (write PNG to a temp path, send t=f with path) and Memory (POSIX shm or anonymous mmap, send t=s) transports, with cleanup, error handling, and fallbacks. Add tests and a CLI flag to select transport.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Triple-Ctrl-C exit guard; single Ctrl-C passes through
description: kittui-wm-demo (and the attach client) treat single Ctrl-C as an InputEvent::Char{ch: '\x03'} forwarded to the focused window. Track a counter that decays 1s after the last Ctrl-C; on count >= 3 within the window, restore terminal and exit. The 'q to quit' message in the footer changes to 'Ctrl-C Ctrl-C Ctrl-C to quit' once any backend is hosting an app that might consume Ctrl-C. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show heading anchor count in kittui-md rich status
description: Update kittui-md rich/interactive status line so heading-anchor coverage is visible alongside heading counts. Add targeted tests and keep changes narrow.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add app discovery commands to native socket
description:
Large gap: no-arg native `kittwm` owns the live DISPLAY-like socket, but it does not support the app discovery commands available on the standalone daemon (`APPS`, `APPS_JSON`, `APPS_FIRST`, `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST`). Inside a native WM session, attach clients should be able to discover launchable commands before calling `SPAWN_PTY`.

Scope:
- Teach `NativeSpawnQueue` to handle `APPS`, `APPS_JSON`, `APPS_FIRST <query>`, and `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST <query>` by reusing existing daemon helpers.
- Keep existing native socket spawn/focus/close/layout/status behavior unchanged.
- Add targeted tests for APPS_JSON and query routing on native queue replies.
- Document the attach commands in docs/wm.md.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm --attach -c APPS_JSON` works against a native session socket.
- Tests cover native queue APPS_JSON and APPS_FIRST responses.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui UI primitives: textbox, headings, title/banner/header/footer, textchip
description: Add reusable kittui UI component primitives so callers do not handroll common surfaces. Components requested: textbox, h1/h2/h3, title, banner, header, footer, textchip. Each should produce kittui scenes/layers with consistent spacing, typography-like scale (within terminal constraints), padding, border/glow/theme hooks, and deterministic sizing. Include snapshot/proof tests and small CLI/example output. Should be usable by markdown renderer and kittwm overlays.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add visible chrome metadata to raw-frame path
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm sessions use the raw RGBA fast path, but raw frames carry only image bytes/footprints. The reusable kittwm chrome theme is only visible on the slower Scene compose path, so live raw sessions lack visible title/focus chrome.

Scope:
- Extend `RawFrame` with lightweight chrome metadata: title string, focused flag, and tiled/floating mode.
- Populate metadata in `Compositor::raw_frames` from focus/mode/window id.
- Render a minimal terminal title strip in `session.rs` above/over each raw frame using the metadata so focused windows are visibly marked.
- Add targeted tests for raw frame metadata.

Acceptance:
- `raw_frames` marks the focused frame and provides title/mode metadata.
- Session code renders title text without affecting image placement semantics.
- `cargo test -p kittui-wm raw_frame` and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Exercise link and image titles in kittui-md proof gallery
description: Update docs/examples/kittui-md-proof.md so sample links/images include Markdown title attributes, validating the newly preserved metadata through --links, --images, and --metadata-json.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon SPAWN creates tracked panes instead of detached processes
description: Next proposal: current SPAWN starts a detached process. Extend the kittwm daemon protocol/session state so socket-only spawn creates a tracked pane/window with layout/focus metadata and can be listed/attached by clients. Builds on bd-cddcf2/bd-624e22.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md modes and metadata outputs
description: Document the kittui-md CLI modes added during Markdown work: --plain, --rich, --interactive, --outline, and --metadata-json, including what metadata JSON contains and where the proof gallery lives. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: kittui-wm hosts an xterm via XQuartz on macOS
description: Integration test that skips if XQuartz isn't installed. Spawns XquartzServer, launches xterm against the display, waits for it to map, calls compose() and asserts at least one non-empty capture is returned. The demo gains --features xquartz to drive this end-to-end with tendril screenshot proof. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md --mode name selector
description: Add a --mode <name> selector for kittui-md so users and tools can choose any output mode by canonical flag or alias name without spelling the dedicated flag. Document it and add parser/conflict/unknown-mode tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: return channelized batch placement JSON
description:
Large gap: platform bindings can batch-place scenes, including at a runtime origin, but only receive one concatenated byte string. CLI users can request channelized JSON (`upload`, `placement`, `embed`, image ids, footprints), while FFI/TypeScript hosts cannot inspect, schedule, or separately write the upload/placement/embed channels.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_place_many_json_channels(runtime, scenes_json, x, y, out)` returning JSON for a batch placed at a group origin.
- Use `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin` and include `count`, `image_ids`, `footprints`, `upload_bytes`, `placement_bytes`, `embed_bytes`, and raw `upload`/`placement`/`embed` strings.
- Bump FFI ABI minor and update `kittui.h` plus ABI snapshot tests.
- Expose `Kittui.placeManyChannels(scenes, x, y)` in TypeScript and add tests.

Acceptance:
- FFI returns parseable JSON with channel strings and metadata for scene arrays.
- TypeScript returns a parsed object.
- Targeted FFI, ABI snapshot, and TypeScript tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Bound headless Chrome native test startup waits

description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm implements insert-character CSI (`CSI n @`) but not terminal insert/replace mode (`CSI 4 h/l`, IRM). Editors and line-oriented TUIs can enable insert mode so printable output shifts existing cells right instead of overwriting them; without it, native pane rendering and READ_TEXT snapshots diverge from real terminals.

Acceptance:

title: Complete kitty graphics protocol conformance + ratakittui coverage
description: Operator-confirmed gap: kittui was supposed to be a careful extraction of the Cacophony TUI graphics lib against https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ but the showcase reproduces ENOENT image-not-found responses in Ghostty and produces only placeholder character grids. Audit shows multiple spec deviations: (1) unicode placeholders emit bare U+10EEEE with foreground color only, missing the (row, column, msb-of-image-id) combining diacritics that the spec requires for cell-to-pixel anchoring; (2) the q= quiet field is never set so kitty prints diagnostic responses inline; (3) declared Transport::File and Transport::Memory never adjust t= and still base64-stream via a=t; (4) animation upload uses a=t with r= instead of a=t for first frame then a=f for subsequent frames and the animation control verb shape is wrong; (5) placement command lacks p= placement-id support and X=/Y= subcell offset support; (6) tests assert substrings of the current malformed output instead of the protocol grammar. This epic tracks getting kittui to a verified spec-conformant baseline plus full ratakittui widget coverage proof. Child beads will land incrementally with grammar-pinned tests and a kittui proof CLI mode the operator can paste into Ghostty/kitty for visual confirmation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm native app: nested PTY terminal panes
description: Operator direction: support a native terminal app independent of X/Quartz. Implement a nested PTY backend for kittui-wm so terminal sessions can be resized, moved, composed as windows, and receive keyboard input, allowing kittwm to act as a tmux-like multiplexer as well as a graphical compositor. Acceptance: launch shell in a PTY, render terminal contents into kittui frames, resize with pane geometry, route typed input, and prove with an echo hello round trip. Parent epic: bd-031e54; directly helps bd-a9ec5b when xterm/XQuartz are unavailable. Filed from bd-a9ec5b session.

title: kittwm: forward OSC 52 clipboard writes from native panes
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm consumes inner PTY OSC sequences while rendering panes itself, so OSC 52 clipboard requests from apps do not reach the host terminal clipboard. A terminal WM should mediate clipboard OSC safely and transparently: inner apps should be able to set the host clipboard via Ghostty's OSC 52 support, while kittwm keeps its own pane model/rendering path.

Scope:
- Recognize OSC 52 clipboard sequences in native `TerminalState`.
- Queue sanitized OSC 52 set-clipboard sequences for host-terminal forwarding instead of rendering them or writing them back to the child PTY.
- Drain host-forwarded OSC sequences from `PtyTerminalApp` in the native session loop and write them to stdout outside pane frames.
- Preserve existing OSC title handling.
- Add focused tests for OSC 52 capture/sanitization and drain behavior.

Acceptance:
- Test proves OSC 52 with base64 payload queues a host OSC 52 sequence.
- Test proves empty/query-like payloads are ignored rather than forwarded as clipboard reads.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.

Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native PTY panes do not track terminal mouse-reporting modes (`?1000`, `?1002`, `?1003`, `?1006`). kittwm needs this state before it can safely route host mouse reports into the focused/target pane, and controllers need to know whether a TUI is asking for mouse input.

Lands the XServer trait implementation now; the CGVirtualDisplayCreate FFI itself behind unsafe extern "C" symbols, with a graceful fallback to capturing the main display when virtual display creation fails. The kittui_wm_demo learns --features quartz to drive it on macOS the same way --features xvfb drives Xvfb on Linux.

title: animation first transmit emits redundant r=1; per-frame delay grammar should use a=t/a=f z= not a=a r=N z=
description: First animation frame uses a=t and currently includes r=1, which is redundant per spec (transmit is implicitly frame 1). Per-frame delays are also emitted as a=a,r=N,z=<ms> control commands; the canonical way is to set z= on the upload (a=t/a=f) command itself. Audit + adjust. Parent: bd-42d7c1.

- Change `Kittui.placeMany(scenes)` to call the batch FFI API once and return the concatenated batch bytes string.
- Add `placeManyList(scenes)` or equivalent only if needed for old per-scene array behavior; keep documented behavior aligned.
- Update TypeScript declarations and README.
- Add fake-library tests proving one batch FFI call is made.

Acceptance:
- `placeMany([scene, scene])` calls `kittui_place_many_json` once with a JSON array.
- Existing `place` and `placeAt` tests still pass.
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` passes.

Add --launcher-query QUERY / KITTUI_WM_LAUNCH_QUERY so Ctrl-A Enter (and other launch actions) select the first matching PATH command or macOS app and launch it, falling back to KITWM_LAUNCH_CMD/xterm when unset. title: Fix animation upload to use a=t/a=f frame appends and a=a control
description:
Animation upload currently uses repeated a=t,r=N which the spec does not document for appending frames. Per https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#animation the first frame uses a=t with f= and i= as usual, subsequent frames use a=f with the same i= to append, and the animation control sets gap/loop count via a=a with parameters s=, c=, z=. Audit kittui-kitty::upload_animation against the spec, fix the verb sequence, set per-frame delays via the appropriate field, add grammar tests, and ensure native looping plays without escape traffic per cycle.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Compositor multiplex: many backends, one session

title: Wire kittui-wm NativeApp surfaces into live compositor panes
description: Follow-up to bd-03e525/bd-3f4b5d: native PTY and headless browser proof commands exist, but kittwm live session still composes XServer-backed Compositor windows only. Add a native-app compositor path that treats NativeFrame::Rgba/PNG surfaces as movable/resizable/focusable kittwm panes, routes keyboard/mouse into the focused NativeApp, and lets the launcher spawn native terminal/browser panes. Parent epic: bd-031e54. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-03e525/bd-3f4b5d session.
