title: kittwm: make float toggle affect compositor window mode
description:
Large gap: kittwm capture-backed sessions expose `float.toggle` in the keymap/footer, but it only updates local status text. The compositor already supports tiled vs floating window modes, so the keybinding should change the focused window's real placement/chrome mode.

Scope:
- Add compositor helpers to resolve the focused-or-first window, query a window mode, and toggle the focused window between floating and tiled.
- Use the same focused-or-first logic in Scene and raw-frame composition so only one default frame/window is focused.
- Wire `Action::FloatToggle` in `session.rs` to the compositor toggle helper while preserving footer status.
- Add targeted compositor tests for mode toggling and Scene default focus uniqueness.

Acceptance:
- `float.toggle` updates the focused window's compositor mode and subsequent frames show tiled/floating mode changes.
- Raw and Scene composition both mark exactly one default focused window.
- Targeted tests plus `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive links toggle
description: Add a native links toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press l to inspect document links without leaving the pager. Reuse existing link metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/link-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support native mouse drag motion events
description:
Mouse routing gap after host mouse routing: motion events are only injected when all-motion (`?1003`) is enabled and are always encoded as no-button motion. TUIs often request button-motion (`?1002`) and expect drag reports with the held button encoded (`32 + button`). Native kittwm should support drag motions without requiring all-motion.

Scope:
- Preserve the held mouse button from `InputEvent::MouseMove` when mapping host events.
- Encode `move-left`, `move-middle`, and `move-right` as SGR button-motion reports gated by `mouse_button_motion` or `mouse_all_motion`.
- Keep no-button hover `move` gated by `mouse_all_motion`.
- Add focused tests for drag/hover encoding and host-event mapping.
- Update docs for accepted `SEND_MOUSE`/routing event names if needed.

Acceptance:
- Tests prove left-drag works with button-motion mode and hover still requires all-motion.
- Targeted kittui-cli tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md outline-only mode for quick heading scans
description: Add a kittui-md --outline mode that parses Markdown and prints only the heading outline metadata, without rendering all components. This gives a fast scan/navigation view for long documents. Include parser and output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md json alias for metadata-json
description: Add --json as a concise alias for kittui-md --metadata-json. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check that the alias emits schema_version.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: add tonal panel command backed by affordances
description:
Large gap: DESIGN and module docs mention `kittui panel`, and `kittui-affordances` already provides tonal panel chrome, but the CLI still lacks a panel command. This blocks shell scripts from using the same native kittui chrome patterns that kittwm/ratakittui can use.

Scope:
- Add `kittui panel --tone assistant|tool|user -w W -h H [--animate]`.
- Build the scene through `kittui_affordances::panel_chrome(...).to_scene(...)` so CLI output matches the reusable affordance layer.
- Support `--scene-json`, `--json`, channel modes, and dry-run via existing emit path.
- Add targeted CLI tests and update README/DESIGN if needed.

Acceptance:
- `kittui panel --tone assistant -w 20 -h 4 --scene-json` emits valid Scene JSON.
- `--animate` creates a scene with animation metadata.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native pane mouse event injection
description:
Native mouse-control gap: kittwm now publishes which panes request mouse reporting, but controllers still have no stable native socket/CLI primitive to send mouse events to a pane. This blocks automation of TUIs that use mouse input and forces raw byte crafting.

Scope:
- Add a native socket command `SEND_MOUSE <window|focused> <event> <col> <row>` where event is `press-left`, `press-middle`, `press-right`, `release`, `move`, `scroll-up`, or `scroll-down`.
- Encode events as SGR mouse reports when the target pane has compatible mouse modes enabled (`mouse_sgr` and basic/all-motion as appropriate); return/log no-op otherwise.
- Add CLI wrapper `kittwm --send-mouse WINDOW EVENT COL ROW`.
- Update HELP/docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover SGR mouse payload encoding and disabled-mode no-op behavior.
- Daemon tests cover parsing/queueing/help.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add POSIX shm allocator for raw-frame transport
description:
Follow-up from bd-67a477. The raw-frame kitty protocol now has file/tempfile/shared-memory grammar and KITTUI_TRANSPORT=file tempfile upload, but POSIX shared-memory allocation is not wired yet. Implement a safe local shared-memory allocator for large raw RGBA frames where supported.

Scope:
- Add a local-only POSIX shm allocation path for raw RGBA uploads when policy selects shared memory.
- Keep tmux/remote defaults safe and fall back to direct/file when shm is unavailable.
- Ensure cleanup/unlink happens reliably.
- Add targeted tests for allocator naming/fallback and protocol grammar without requiring terminal support.

Acceptance:
- Local shm raw-frame upload path exists behind explicit policy/env selection.
- Unsupported platforms/failures fall back safely.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native PTY row and column split layouts
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes can split, but every split uses the same side-by-side column layout. A terminal WM needs both horizontal and vertical tiling orientations so users can choose editor-over-shell or side-by-side layouts.

Scope:
- Track a native pane layout axis in the no-arg `kittwm` session.
- Map `Ctrl-A %` / `Ctrl-A |` / `Ctrl-A v` to side-by-side columns and `Ctrl-A -` / `Ctrl-A "` / `Ctrl-A h` to stacked rows.
- Recompute per-pane title/app geometry for either axis on split, resize, close, and socket spawn.
- Update footer/docs and targeted layout tests.

Acceptance:
- Native pane layout tests cover both columns and rows.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document Python binding as first-party platform surface
description:
Large gap: README/DESIGN still describe Python bindings as future or only mention the C ABI generically, even though the first-party Python ctypes binding now exists. This makes the platform story stale for shell/external automation users.

Scope:
- Update README shipped surfaces and quick-start examples to include `bindings/python` and `python -m kittui`.
- Update DESIGN FFI/bindings section so Python is a committed first-party ctypes binding, not future work.
- Mention channelized batch placement and module discovery path.

Acceptance:
- Docs accurately describe Rust/CLI/C/TS/Python binding surfaces.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: expose semantic snapshot socket skeleton
description:
Follow-up from bd-911832/bd-6c4bc5. Expose the first native socket semantic snapshot/action/focus command skeletons so SDK clients have a control-plane target for semantic surfaces. Start conservatively by adapting existing terminal pane status/text into a semantic snapshot and returning explicit unsupported errors for action/focus until real semantic adapters exist.

Scope:
- Add native socket commands and HELP/HELP_JSON entries for SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT <window|focused>, SEMANTIC_ACTION <window|focused> <component> <action> <json>, and SEMANTIC_FOCUS <window|focused> <component>.
- SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT should return JSON using the SDK semantic protocol shape, with existing terminal pane text represented as a text_area/terminal-like node.
- Action/focus commands should validate shape and return clear unsupported errors rather than pretending to mutate terminal UI semantics.
- Add targeted daemon tests.

Acceptance:
- Semantic snapshot command returns stable JSON for focused/native pane.
- HELP_JSON lists semantic commands.
- Unsupported actions/focus return explicit ERR lines.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Markdown-to-kittui renderer using UI primitives
description: Implement a markdown renderer that parses markdown and emits kittui UI components rather than plain terminal text. Required blocks: headings, paragraphs/textboxes, blockquotes/callouts, lists, code blocks, thematic breaks, tables, and links. Links should render as highlighted chips. Renderer should preserve enough layout metadata to compose backgrounds/overlays using kitty placement semantics. Include tests for representative markdown and a CLI/example path to render a markdown file.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add categories to kittui-md mode discovery JSON
description: Extend kittui-md mode discovery JSON surfaces so modes carry a category such as render, inspect, json, discovery, or stats. Include categories in --modes-json, --mode-info-json, and --mode-search-json. Document it and add output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add fuzz targets and workspace integration smoke from DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md calls for cargo-fuzz targets for Scene::from_json and CLI flag parsers plus a workspace integration smoke test that builds a runtime, renders a small scene, verifies PNG bytes, and checks placement escapes. Current repo has unit/golden/parity/ABI tests, but no fuzz/ directory and no root tests/it.rs smoke. Add bounded fuzz targets and a fast integration smoke that can run in CI without a real kitty terminal.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: add fuzz targets and integration tests

title: kittwm: investigate dirty-grid kitty frame updates
description:
Performance exploration: kitty's animation protocol can update image frames and may support lower-bandwidth/diff-like rendering strategies, but using it as a WM dirty-region transport could be subtle/buggy. Investigate and prototype a kittwm dirty-grid/delta frame transport that tracks changed regions per surface, updates only dirty regions or animation frames where safe, and falls back to full raw-RGBA uploads.

Scope:
- Research kitty graphics animation/frame update semantics and whether partial/delta updates are possible/safe for kittwm surfaces.
- Design a dirty-grid over native surface frames with configurable granularity and full-frame fallback.
- Prototype behind an opt-in env/config flag only.
- Include correctness tests for dirty-region detection and fallback behavior.
- Document risks around flicker, stale pixels, tmux passthrough, and terminal compatibility.

Acceptance:
- In-repo plan/prototype explains whether animation frame updates or other kitty protocol features are suitable.
- No default behavior changes unless proven safe.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: publish native pane geometry in socket status
description:
Large gap: native kittwm PANES_JSON reports pane identity/focus/title/weight, but not the actual terminal geometry. Socket clients cannot inspect where panes are or how weights resolved into cells, which limits external controllers and chrome/preview tools.

Scope:
- Extend native pane status with optional layout geometry: title cell rect and app cell rect.
- Publish geometry from the native PTY loop after weighted layouts are computed.
- Include geometry in PANES text/JSON and STATUS_JSON pane details without breaking existing fields.
- Add targeted tests for status serialization with geometry.

Acceptance:
- PANES_JSON includes x/y/cols/rows/app_x/app_y/app_cols/app_rows when a native session publishes layouts.
- Text PANES includes layout geometry.
- Existing native daemon/session tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md anchors inspection mode
description: Add --anchors as a focused output mode that prints heading level, anchor slug, and heading text from kittui-md outlines. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm Compositor + Floating layout + LifecycleTracker integration
description:
Add Compositor type owning a kittui::Runtime, LifecycleTracker, and a window-id -> placement-id map. Adds LayoutNode::Floating { rect_px, draggable } and a per-frame composite() that turns Xvfb window snapshots into Scene::Image placements with chrome (border, glow, scanlines from the existing ratakittui design), then drains the LifecycleTracker so closed X windows emit kitty deletes.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket pane move command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes can be spawned, focused, closed, renamed, and have their layout axis changed over the socket, but there is no socket/keymap way to reorder panes within the layout. As a terminal WM, pane order is core window-management state.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `MOVE_PANE <window|focused> <left|right|up|down|first|last>`.
- Queue and handle the command in the native PTY loop by reordering the pane vector and recomputing layout, preserving focus on the moved pane.
- Include the command in HELP/HELP_JSON and error text.
- Add targeted parser and pure reorder helper tests.

Acceptance:
- Socket command queues and drains a typed move command.
- Native pane move helper clamps at ends and preserves focus on the moved pane.
- `cargo test -p kittui-cli daemon::tests::native_spawn_queue_accepts_control_commands -- --nocapture`, native move helper test, and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show heading anchor count in kittui-md stats
description: Update kittui-md --stats/--summary so concise metadata output reports heading_anchors alongside headings, making the anchor feature visible in text summaries. Add targeted tests and docs.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add zlib compression mode for graphics uploads
description:
Transport efficiency gap: kittui/kittwm raw kitty graphics uploads send full base64 RGBA frames, which is extremely expensive over local terminal streams and especially dangerous through tmux passthrough. Kitty graphics supports zlib compression via the `o=z` field. Add a global opt-in/auto compression mode for kitty uploads, with native kittwm using it for raw frame uploads when graphics are enabled.

Scope:
- Add zlib-compressed direct upload support to kittui-kitty for PNG/direct bytes and raw RGBA (`f=32`) payloads, using kitty `o=z` grammar.
- Add runtime/env selection for graphics compression (e.g. `KITTUI_KITTY_COMPRESSION=zlib|none|auto`).
- Use compression for native kittwm raw frame uploads outside tmux when beneficial/selected.
- Add grammar tests proving `o=z` appears and payload round-trips through zlib/base64 decode.
- Document that outside tmux graphics are emitted as terminal escape sequences by default, not socket transport, unless a file/shared-memory transport is selected elsewhere.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover zlib upload grammar for raw RGBA.
- Existing uncompressed upload grammar remains available.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm launch: spawn xterm or arbitrary command and report pid
description: Small launcher slice under bd-031e54 / bd-8d64b1. Add 'kitwm launch [--] [CMD ARGS...]' as a safe visible operator capability: default command is xterm, otherwise spawns the provided command, prints pid + argv, and exits. This proves launch plumbing before the in-session Mod+Return floating launcher UI. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket pane text snapshots
description:
Large terminal-WM control-plane gap: external kittwm controllers can inject text/keys but cannot read the current native PTY screen without scraping kitty output. A terminal WM needs a socket-level pane text snapshot for automation, accessibility, tests, and controller feedback loops.

Scope:
- Publish per-pane text snapshots from the native PTY loop to the in-process native socket queue without adding the full text to PANES_JSON.
- Add socket commands:
  - `READ_TEXT <window|focused>` returns a multi-line text snapshot ending with `END`.
  - `READ_TEXT_JSON <window|focused>` returns `{window, text}` JSON.
- Support `focused` using the live focused pane metadata.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON/error coverage and documentation examples.

Acceptance:
- READ_TEXT/READ_TEXT_JSON validate a window target and return clear errors if no pane matches.
- client_request_multi can read READ_TEXT output.
- Targeted daemon/native tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: examples: add composite app using terminal and browser surfaces
description: Add an example SDK app that spawns a terminal surface and browser/GUI surface, composes frames side-by-side, and routes input with coordinate offsets to validate reusable WM primitives.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md defaults discovery outputs
description: Add --defaults and --defaults-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query default mode, width range, stdin/file input behavior, and interactive defaults. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render semantic component surfaces via kittui affordances
description:
Implementation gap: once semantic component surfaces exist, kittwm needs a renderer bridge that turns component trees into the same kittui primitives/chrome used by kittwm. This should let semantic SDK/toolkit surfaces render through pure terminal, kitty graphics, kittui scene, or remote/headless outputs without bespoke per-app drawing.

Scope:
- Define a kittwm semantic-surface renderer adapter that consumes component trees and produces kittui primitive scenes/chrome model output.
- Reuse first-party kittui-affordances controls instead of duplicating button/radio/text-input rendering in kittwm.
- Document/implement fallback behavior when a semantic node cannot be represented exactly.
- Add a small synthetic semantic surface test/example.

Acceptance:
- Synthetic component tree can render through kittui primitives with focus/selection state preserved.
- kittwm renderer bridge uses shared affordance components.
- No high-level UI controls are added to kittui-core.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md about discovery outputs
description: Add --about and --about-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can inspect the binary name, package version, default mode, and high-level capabilities. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive reload key
description: Add a native reload key to kittui-md --interactive so users can refresh the Markdown file in-place after editing it. Document the keybinding, expose it through keybinding discovery, and add targeted tests for the pager action and reload helper.

description: Add a stable schema_version field to kittui-md --metadata-json so downstream consumers can detect future JSON shape changes. Add a test asserting the schema version.

title: Preserve Markdown link and image titles
description: Preserve pulldown-cmark link/image title attributes in kittui-affordances metadata and expose them through kittui-md JSON and focused link/image modes. Add targeted tests and docs if needed.

title: kitwm --attach REPL + WINDOWS/DISPLAYS protocol verbs
description: Extend daemon protocol with WINDOWS (lists Mac windows) and DISPLAYS (lists displays), and add 'kitwm --attach' that opens an interactive line-oriented REPL against the running daemon. REPL accepts PING/STATUS/WINDOWS/DISPLAYS/QUIT/help and prints replies; exits on Ctrl-D. Makes --attach real (no longer placeholder). Parent: bd-031e54 (decomposes bd-aaea73).
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add nix flake
description: Add a nix flake to the project. See tendril and other rust caco projects as examples for implementation details and best practices.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kittwm: support native PTY cursor save and restore
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm handles many cursor movement sequences but not cursor save/restore. Shell prompts and TUIs commonly use DECSC/DECRC (`ESC 7` / `ESC 8`) or SCO-style `CSI s` / `CSI u` around status updates; without support, subsequent output can land in the wrong cell and corrupt READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Track a saved cursor in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `ESC 7` / `ESC 8` and `CSI s` / `CSI u`.
- Preserve saved cursor sensibly across resize by clamping.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Tests demonstrate saving, moving, writing, restoring, and writing at the saved position for both ESC and CSI variants.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm record: dump N capture frames to PNG sequence for debugging
description: kitwm record [--frames N] [--out DIR] runs the chosen backend for N frames, writes each capture as PNG into DIR (default /tmp/kitwm-record-<timestamp>/), prints the directory path. Useful for: visually verifying the capture pipeline without TCC permissions on the WM terminal; bug reports; making demo gifs. Works with --capture SPEC. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md: standalone rich kitty-graphics markdown viewer
description: Operator clarification: the markdown renderer should ship as a full kittui Markdown viewer, usable both as a kittwm-native app and directly in a normal terminal without `kittwm replace`. Add a `kittui-md` (or `kittui md`) viewer binary/command that reads a Markdown file/stdin, renders it with rich kittui/kitty graphics using the UI component layer, and supports normal terminal use even outside kittwm. When launched inside kittwm it can use KITTWM_SOCKET/KITTWM_WINDOW context for future interactivity, but it must not require that context. Include navigation/scrolling plan, terminal size handling, and smoke tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md definitions-only mode for glossary inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --definitions mode that prints only parsed definition-list entries with term and definition text. This complements --tables and --code-blocks for focused inspections. Include output tests.

description:

description: Add stable index fields to remaining kittui-md metadata-json array records: outline, footnotes, definitions, math, html, metadata_blocks, code_blocks, and tables. Keep scalar footnote_references unchanged. Add targeted tests and docs.

title: bindings-python: add ctypes platform binding
description:
Large gap: kittui can now be used from Rust, CLI, C, and TypeScript, but Python shell/platform automation still has to hand-roll ctypes calls to the C ABI. Python is a common glue layer for terminal tooling and external platforms.

Scope:
- Add a small first-party Python ctypes binding under `bindings/python`.
- Support runtime config, ABI version, probe, unplace, place, place_at, place_many, place_many_at, and place_many_channels.
- Include pure stdlib unittest coverage using a fake CDLL so the API is validated without requiring a built shared library.
- Add a README with usage examples.

Acceptance:
- `python3 -m unittest discover bindings/python` passes.
- Binding can normalize scene dicts/JSON strings and parse channel JSON.
- No changes to Rust ABI are required.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm runtime keymap: Ctrl-A prefix actions for launch/quit/focus placeholders
description: Wire the keymap config foundation into the live session. title: kittui-cli: expose kittwm chrome scene command
description:

- Flags: `--title`, `--focused`, `--floating`, `-w/--width`, `-h/--height`.
- Support existing global modes (`--scene-json`, `--json`, render/place paths) through the normal emit pipeline.
- Add targeted CLI unit coverage.

Acceptance:

- Targeted kittui CLI tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.

title: Add kittui-md counts inspection mode

title: kitwm: flicker between frames — drop \x1b[H\x1b[J + only re-emit changed windows
description: Operator: 'image flickers between frames. should be a smooth consistent WM experience, only insides of windows are latency bound but can't cause main WM to flicker'. Cause: session::run_loop writes \x1b[H\x1b[J every frame to clear, then re-emits each window's upload+placement+placeholder. The clear wipes the previous frame before the new pixels arrive over the slow tmux passthrough, so the operator sees a black flash per frame. Fix: stop clearing the screen, kitty atomically replaces an image at the same id; only emit placement+placeholder on first frame per window or when footprint changes; never re-emit when nothing changed; track last-seen window set so removed windows get a single delete escape. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: WM-thread architecture: per-backend Pump (capture+encode), non-blocking UI tick
description: Move capture + PNG/raw-RGB encode off the UI thread. Each attached backend becomes a Pump with its own worker thread that loops capture -> encode -> store-in-slot. MultiCompositor::tick(layout) reads slots non-blockingly and returns whatever's there. UI thread keeps running input, resize, layout, hit-test at 60fps regardless of slow windows. Slow windows just show their previous frame until they catch up.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make fullscreen toggle affect compositor placement
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm exposes `fullscreen.toggle`, but it is still only footer state. As a terminal WM, fullscreen should change the focused window's real compositor placement/chrome, just like the recently wired focus and float actions.

Scope:
- Add compositor fullscreen state and helpers for focused-or-first fullscreen toggle/query.
- Add `Layout::bounds()` so fullscreen can occupy the tiled layout envelope when available.
- Make raw and Scene composition use the fullscreen target rect before tiled/floating mode.
- Carry fullscreen metadata in `RawFrame` and show it in the raw terminal chrome label.
- Wire `Action::FullscreenToggle` in `session.rs` to compositor fullscreen state while preserving footer text.
- Add targeted compositor tests for fullscreen target footprint and toggling.

Acceptance:
- `fullscreen.toggle` updates the focused window's compositor fullscreen state.
- Raw frames report fullscreen metadata and use layout bounds for focused fullscreen windows.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description:
Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process ("server") and a thin viewer process ("client"), communicating over a Unix domain socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux's $TMUX or X11's $DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux's pane sharing) — useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock).\n\nMakes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.

---

title: Implement deterministic Scene normalization before hashing

title: kittwm: support native PTY scroll regions
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm scrolls the entire screen whenever output reaches the bottom row. Full-screen TUIs and pagers commonly set DEC scroll margins with `CSI top;bottom r` so only a viewport/body region scrolls while headers/status bars remain fixed. Without this, rendered panes and READ_TEXT snapshots corrupt status lines.

Scope:
- Track a vertical scroll region in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI top;bottom r`, including reset/defaults.
- Make newline-at-bottom scroll only the active region and keep content outside the region fixed.
- Clamp/reset margins sensibly across resize and alternate-screen creation.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test shows scrolling inside a region preserves header/footer lines.
- Test shows `CSI r` reset returns to whole-screen scrolling.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Encode kitty unicode-placeholder diacritics per spec
description:
placeholder_text in crates/kittui-kitty currently emits bare U+10EEEE characters with only a \x1b[38:2:r:g:b]m foreground color set to the low 24 bits of the image id. The kitty graphics spec (https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#unicode-placeholders) requires every placeholder cell to carry up to three combining diacritics encoding (row, column, most-significant-byte-of-image-id). Without these, kitty/Ghostty treat every cell as row 0 column 0 of the image and the placement does not anchor. Implement diacritic encoding from the spec's tables, support image ids whose top byte is non-zero, add exact-grammar unit tests, and snapshot the placeholder grid for a known fixture.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

Acceptance:

- Supported terminal overrides continue to place scenes.

title: kittwm: restore native sessions from manifest JSON
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: kittwm can now export a persistence-oriented `SESSION_JSON` manifest, but there is no native socket restore path. Session persistence remains half-built until a controller can hand the manifest back to the WM and have it recreate pane order, layout, titles, commands, weights, and focus.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON <json>` accepting the `SESSION_JSON` shape.
- Queue a native restore command that replaces current native panes with newly spawned PTYs from manifest commands.
- Restore layout axis, pane order, display titles, weights, and focused pane index.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON coverage and docs example.

Acceptance:
- Daemon parser validates restore manifests and queues one restore command.
- Native loop applies the restore command and redraws.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: enforce native surface frame bounds on layout resize
description:
Native WM resize/layout bug: switching native panes between columns and rows can flicker badly because a pane may capture/render at its previous full logical size during the resize transition, while the WM places it into a smaller layout. The WM must enforce each window's frame bounds regardless of backend timing: resize the surface to its new logical cell area, but also crop/pad captured frames to the allocated pixel footprint before placement. Scaling/zooming should remain a separate future feature, not implicit pane resize behavior.

Scope:
- Add a native-session frame fitting/cropping helper for RGBA frames using current cell dimensions (8x16 today) and allocated app_cols/app_rows.
- Before kitty placement, ensure frame dimensions exactly match the allocated native pane footprint in pixels; crop overflow and pad underflow with terminal background.
- Preserve normal PTY resize calls so apps are still informed of new logical cols/rows.
- Add focused tests proving oversized frames are cropped and undersized frames are padded without scaling.
- Document resize semantics: WM reports logical size to surfaces and enforces bounds; zoom/scaling is separate future behavior.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover crop and pad behavior.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: macOS quickstart with XQuartz install + Nix integration

Update docs/wm.md macOS section: brew install --cask xquartz, then cargo run --release -p kittui-cli --example kittui_wm_demo --features quartz,xquartz. Include a Nix fragment that adds /opt/X11/bin to PATH for the devshell on Darwin. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: kittwm: implement native PTY erase CSI modes
description:
Large terminal fidelity gap: native PTY rendering treats all CSI `J` erase-display modes as clear-screen and all CSI `K` erase-line modes as erase-to-end. Real terminal apps use `CSI 1K`, `CSI 2K`, `CSI 0J`, and `CSI 1J`; incorrect erase semantics can corrupt native pane rendering and automation READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Implement CSI `K` modes 0/1/2: cursor-to-end, start-to-cursor, whole line.
- Implement CSI `J` modes 0/1/2: cursor-to-end-of-screen, start-of-screen-to-cursor, whole screen.
- Keep default mode semantics (`0`) unchanged.
- Add focused TerminalState parser tests.

Acceptance:
- Text snapshots reflect correct erase-line/display modes.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

Chips must compose cleanly inside paragraphs/textboxes and wrap predictably across terminal widths.

title: kittwm: add native socket relative focus commands
description:
Large gap: native kittwm socket clients can focus panes by explicit window token, but cannot perform relative focus navigation. Scripts/controllers first have to query PANES/PANES_JSON and compute the next window themselves, unlike the in-session keyboard focus cycle.

Scope:

- Drain/apply them in the native session loop using existing focus model.
- Include commands in HELP/HELP_JSON and docs.
- Add queue parsing tests and pure focus helper tests.

- Focus helper wraps correctly for multiple panes and handles empty/one-pane cases.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.

title: kittwm: publish native pane cursor position
description:
Large terminal automation gap: native pane status and READ_TEXT expose screen contents but not cursor position. Controllers cannot tell where a prompt/caret is, and external previews cannot draw cursor-aware chrome. The PTY parser already tracks cursor row/col, but it is not published.

Scope:
- Expose `PtyTerminalApp::cursor_position()` from native terminal state.
- Add optional `cursor_col` / `cursor_row` to `NativePaneStatus`, PANES_JSON, and STATUS_JSON pane details.
- Include cursor position in text `PANES` output.
- Add tests and docs mention.

Acceptance:
- Native pane status includes cursor fields when available.

title: kittwm: publish native pane pid and command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm status reports window/title/focus/weight/geometry, but not the underlying PTY child PID or command. Controllers cannot correlate panes with OS processes for monitoring, cleanup, or external tooling.

Scope:
- Expose `PtyTerminalApp::process_id()` from portable-pty child metadata.
- Store command and pid on native panes when spawned.
- Add optional `pid` and `command` fields to `NativePaneStatus` and PANES text/JSON/status JSON.

Acceptance:

- Existing native tests pass.

title: kittwm: implement native PTY terminal reset controls
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not honor terminal reset controls. Shells/TUIs can emit RIS (`ESC c`) or DECSTR soft reset (`CSI ! p`) to recover terminal state; without support, stale modes such as origin mode, disabled autowrap, hidden cursor, bracketed paste, mouse reporting, or SGR style can leak into later output and corrupt rendering/snapshots.

Scope:
- Implement full reset for `ESC c` (RIS): clear visible cells/scrollback, reset cursor/saved cursor/style, reset scroll region, and restore default terminal modes.
- Implement DECSTR soft reset for `CSI ! p`: reset modes/style/scroll region/cursor state without clearing visible text.
- Preserve existing title behavior unless a test indicates otherwise.
- Add focused parser snapshot/state tests.

Acceptance:
- Tests show RIS clears text and resets modes/style.
- Tests show DECSTR preserves text but resets modes/style and scroll region.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-python: expose render_json PNG bytes
description:
Large gap: the Python binding exposes placement APIs but not the lower-level `kittui_render_json` API. External Python platforms may need PNG bytes for non-terminal previews, tests, or embedding without emitting kitty placement escapes.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_render_json` and `kittui_bytes_free` in the Python ctypes binding.
- Add `Kittui.render(scene) -> bytes` that returns PNG bytes and frees the FFI byte buffer.
- Add fake-CDLL unittest coverage for render success and failure detail.
- Update Python README API list/example.

Acceptance:
- Python tests pass without a real shared library.
- `render` accepts dict/JSON scenes and returns bytes.
- FFI byte buffer ownership is released through `kittui_bytes_free`.

title: kittwm: add send-file byte injection wrapper
description:
Large automation usability gap: `SEND_BYTES_B64` can inject exact bytes, but users still have to run an external base64 encoder and quote the result. Pasting files/scripts/binary payloads into native panes should be first-class from the kittwm CLI.

Scope:

- Read bytes from file or stdin, base64 encode internally, and send `SEND_BYTES_B64` via the default socket.
- Preserve exact bytes; reject invalid window tokens.

title: Add kittui-md exit code discovery outputs
description: Add --exit-codes and --exit-codes-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query exit code meanings independently of README text. Document it and add parser/output tests.

title: Complete terminal transport probing and File/Memory transfer modes
description: DESIGN.md specifies TerminalInfo::detect(), automatic tmux detection, and kitty File/Memory transports (`a=t,t=f` and `a=t,t=s`) in addition to Direct/TmuxPassthrough. Current TerminalInfo only provides defaults/overrides, and kittui-kitty treats File/Memory like direct chunked base64. Implement environment probing over TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/KITTY_WINDOW_ID/KITTY_PUBLIC_KEY/TMUX/WT_SESSION, CLI transport override, file-transfer emission, shared-memory transfer where supported with safe fallback, and tests for all transport encodings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: honor DEC auto-wrap mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm approximates line wrapping but does not honor DEC auto-wrap mode (`CSI ? 7 h/l`). Some terminal apps disable wrapping while drawing fixed-width regions/status cells; native panes currently still wrap when additional printable characters arrive past the right edge, corrupting the next line and READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Track DEC auto-wrap mode in native `TerminalState`, default enabled.
- Handle `CSI ? 7 h` / `CSI ? 7 l`.
- When auto-wrap is disabled, printable characters at/past the last column should overwrite/clamp at the last column instead of moving to the next line.
- Preserve existing default wrapping behavior.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test proves default wrap still advances to next line.
- Test proves disabled auto-wrap keeps writes on the last column.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md limits discovery outputs
description: Add --limits and --limits-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query numeric limits such as width bounds, terminal default clamps, and offset minimum independently of defaults. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: honor native PTY focus reporting
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: kittwm runs multiple native PTY panes but does not honor terminal focus reporting (`CSI ? 1004 h/l`). Terminal apps that enable focus events expect `ESC[I` on focus and `ESC[O` on blur; without this, nested shells/editors cannot react when the WM changes focused pane.

Scope:
- Track focus-reporting mode in native `TerminalState` and expose it through `PtyTerminalApp`.
- When native WM focus changes (keyboard and socket focus commands, close/remove fallback, spawn focus), send focus-out to the previous focused pane and focus-in to the new pane only when the app has enabled focus reporting.
- Add focused tests for mode tracking and focus payload generation.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves `CSI ? 1004 h/l` tracking.
- Unit test proves focus payloads are emitted only for enabled reporting and only on focus changes.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md mode search discovery
description: Add --mode-search <query> and --mode-search-json <query> no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can find modes by flag, alias, or description. Document it and add parser/output tests.

title: kittwm: add native pane balance command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes now have weights and resize controls, but there is no first-class way to rebalance a layout back to equal weights. A terminal WM needs a quick balance/reset operation after manual resizing.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `BALANCE_PANES` queued into the native PTY loop.
- Handle it by resetting all pane weights to 1, recomputing layout, and redrawing.
- Add a local prefix keybinding (`Ctrl-A b`) for balance.
- Include command in HELP/HELP_JSON and parser tests.
- Add/extend tests for balance/reset helper behavior.

Acceptance:
- Socket `BALANCE_PANES` queues and drains a typed command.
- Native session can reset pane weights to equal values locally and over socket.
- Targeted daemon/session tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: answer basic terminal DSR and CPR queries
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm parses app output but does not answer basic Device Status Report queries. Many TUIs probe `CSI 5 n` and `CSI 6 n` expecting terminal OK / cursor-position responses; without CPR, apps can block, mis-detect cursor placement, or fall back to degraded terminal paths.

Scope:
- Add a pending terminal-response path from `TerminalState` back to the PTY writer.
- Handle `CSI 5 n` with `ESC[0n`.
- Handle `CSI 6 n` with `ESC[row;colR` using current 1-based cursor coordinates.
- Ensure the reader loop flushes responses to the child PTY promptly while preserving existing socket/send paths.
- Add focused unit tests for DSR/CPR response bytes.

Acceptance:
- Tests prove `CSI 5n` and `CSI 6n` queue the correct response bytes.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: expose adaptive graphics transport diagnostics
description: Expose adaptive graphics transport diagnostics in kittui/kittwm. Scope: report the selected transport, compression mode, fallback reason, local-vs-remote/tmux classification, and override source through kittwm inspection surfaces and a testable policy selector. Follow-up from bd-3c0dd1.

title: kittwm: wire swap actions to compositor z-order
description:
Large gap: swap/raise-style keybindings in capture-backed kittwm still only update footer counters. Now that hit-testing has deterministic render order, the compositor should expose real z-order operations so keyboard actions can raise/lower the focused window.

Scope:
- Add compositor z-order overrides used by raw and Scene composition order.
- Add helpers to raise/lower the focused-or-first window one step or to top/bottom.
- Wire swap keymap actions in session: right/down raise one step, left/up lower one step while preserving footer text.
- Add targeted tests proving composition order and hit-testing change after raise/lower.

Acceptance:
- Swap keybindings affect actual render/hit-test z-order in capture-backed sessions.
- `raise_focused`/`lower_focused` wrap/clamp deterministically.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: use dirty grid to skip unchanged raw frame uploads
description:
Follow-up from bd-510a36. Use the new kittui-wm dirty-grid helper to skip raw kitty frame uploads when a bounded RGBA frame is byte-identical at tile level. Keep behavior conservative: no partial updates and no visual changes when pixels differ.

Scope:
- Add opt-in `KITTWM_DIRTY_FRAMES=skip-unchanged` (or equivalent renderer policy) around the native raw-frame path.
- Maintain current full-frame delete+upload behavior whenever any tile changed or dimensions changed.
- Add tests proving identical frames skip upload and changed frames still upload normally.
- Keep tmux pure-terminal default unchanged.

Acceptance:
- Opt-in unchanged-frame skipping works for raw RGBA frames.
- No dirty-rectangle/overlay behavior is enabled by default.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.

title: kittui-cli: add render-only PNG command

title: kittui-wm v1: Xvfb-backed WM rendering app windows as floating/tiling chrome
description: Operator direction: kittui-wm becomes a real terminal window manager that hosts Xvfb (or per-app Xephyr) in the agent process tree, captures each X window's framebuffer through SHM, composites them as floating or tiling kittui chrome inside the user's terminal, and routes kitty pointer/keyboard events back to the X server via XTestFake* so the hosted apps respond to clicks and keys. This is the substrate that turns kittui into a full WM over SSH: PATH inherits, env inherits, so anything spawned inside the nested X session picks up the operator's environment. v1 scope: (1) kittui-input parses kitty's mouse + key reporting; (2) kittui-xvfb spawns Xvfb, claims a DISPLAY, captures the root window via MIT-SHM and exposes per-window rects; (3) kittui-wm gains a Compositor that owns a kittui::Runtime + LifecycleTracker, places one Image scene per X window, and feeds pointer events back through XTest; (4) tiling and floating layouts implemented on top of LayoutNode::{Split,Stack,Tab,Floating}; (5) demo example that launches Xvfb + a small X app and renders it as a kittui window. Out of scope for v1: Wayland support, GPU passthrough, raw RGB(A) transmission, multi-display.

title: kittwm: implement native PTY index and reverse-index controls
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not implement terminal index/reverse-index controls (`ESC D`, `ESC E`, `ESC M` and C1 NEL/RI equivalents). TUIs and prompts use reverse index to insert/scroll at the top of a scroll region; missing it can corrupt snapshots and fixed headers.

Scope:
- Add index/next-line/reverse-index helpers to native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `ESC D` (IND), `ESC E` (NEL), `ESC M` (RI) plus C1 `0x84`, `0x85`, `0x8d` if surfaced by vte execute.
- Reverse index should scroll the active region down when the cursor is at `scroll_top`; otherwise move cursor up.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with and without scroll regions.

Acceptance:
- Tests show reverse-index inside a scroll region preserves outside lines and scrolls region downward.
- Tests show next-line moves to column 0 on the next line.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.

title: kittwm: support DEC origin mode in native PTY
description:

Scope:
- Track DEC origin mode in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI ? 6 h` / `CSI ? 6 l`, resetting cursor to home on toggle.
- When origin mode is enabled, make `CSI H/f` row coordinates relative to `scroll_top` and clamped within `scroll_top..scroll_bottom`.
- Reset origin mode on full terminal reset if such helper exists; otherwise preserve existing behavior narrowly.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with scroll region + origin mode.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `CSI 1;1H` inside origin mode writes at scroll-region top, not absolute row 0.
- Tests show disabling origin mode restores absolute addressing.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description:
Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process (server) and a thin viewer process (client), communicating over a Unix domain socket at KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux TMUX or X11 DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux pane sharing) - useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock). Makes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.

title: kittwm: support native PTY alternate screen
description:

title: kittui: separate render scene from terminal placement

Acceptance:

- Existing `Runtime::place` behavior remains unchanged.

description: Add --notes as a friendly alias for kittui-md --footnotes so users can inspect Markdown footnote references and definitions with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support extended SGR colors in native PTY renderer
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap after basic SGR support: many modern shells and TUIs use 256-color and truecolor SGR sequences (`38;5;n`, `48;5;n`, `38;2;r;g;b`, `48;2;r;g;b`). Native kittwm currently preserves only the 16 ANSI colors, so many prompts/status bars still lose their intended colors.

Scope:
- Extend native `TerminalState` SGR parsing to handle semicolon and colon/subparameter forms for:
  - `38;5;n` / `48;5;n` 256-color foreground/background.
  - `38;2;r;g;b` / `48;2;r;g;b` truecolor foreground/background.
- Keep existing 16-color, reset/default, bold, and reverse behavior.
- Add parser/render tests for 256-color and truecolor cells.

Acceptance:
- Tests show 256-color and truecolor SGR are stored on cells and affect RGBA output.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix kitty upload chunk syntax causing Ghostty ENOENT in showcase
description: Harry reported cargo run -p kittui-cli --example showcase in Ghostty prints placeholder glyphs plus kitty responses like Gi=<id>;ENOENT: image not found. Inspection found kittui-kitty encode_chunked emits upload commands as ESC_G,a=t,... with a leading comma before a=t; placement commands use ESC_Ga=p... and are parsed, so Ghostty reports placements for images whose uploads were ignored. Fix upload chunk control syntax, add regression coverage, and verify kittui-kitty tests. Filed from operator report.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add render-many-to-directory helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: FFI/Python/TypeScript can return render-many manifests with base64 PNGs, and the CLI can write batch PNGs to a directory, but platform bindings do not provide the equivalent file-output helper. External hosts have to manually decode base64 manifests and invent naming/manifest conventions.

Scope:
- Add Python `Kittui.render_many_to_dir(scenes, out_dir, prefix="scene")` helper.
- Add TypeScript `Kittui.renderManyToDir(scenes, outDir, options?)` helper.
- Write deterministic files `prefix-00000.png`, etc. and a `manifest.json` with filenames.
- Keep using existing `render_many` / `renderMany` FFI manifest, no ABI change.
- Add binding tests and README updates.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS helpers decode `png_base64` and write files/manifest.
- Existing Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add primitive glow and scanline helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: Python/TypeScript helpers cover solid, gradient, and image primitives, but core effect primitives (`Glow`, `Scanlines`) still require hand-authored Scene JSON. These effects are useful for shell previews and kittwm/chrome-like external renderers.

Scope:
- Add Python `scene.glow_layer(...)`, `scene.glow_box(...)`, `scene.scanlines_layer(...)`, `scene.scanlines_box(...)` helpers.
- Add TypeScript `scene.glowLayer(...)`, `scene.glowBox(...)`, `scene.scanlinesLayer(...)`, `scene.scanlinesBox(...)` helpers.
- Keep helpers primitive-only and JSON-compatible.
- Add binding tests and README examples.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS users can build valid Glow and Scanlines scenes without hand-authoring schema.
- Python and TS tests pass.

title: kittwm: honor application cursor-key mode in native panes
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not honor DECCKM application cursor-key mode (`CSI ? 1 h/l`). Full-screen TUIs commonly enable it and expect arrow keys as SS3 `ESC O A/B/C/D`; native host-key routing and socket status currently ignore the mode, so navigation can be misinterpreted.

Scope:
- Track application cursor-key mode in native `TerminalState`, default false.
- Handle `CSI ? 1 h` / `CSI ? 1 l`.
- Expose the mode from `PtyTerminalApp` and native pane status/JSON/text listings.
- Route parsed host arrow key events to the focused native pane as SS3 arrows when the pane has application cursor mode enabled, otherwise preserve normal CSI arrows/raw behavior.
- Add focused tests for mode tracking and payload selection.

Acceptance:
- Tests show the mode toggles via parser sequences.
- Tests show arrow payloads switch between CSI and SS3 forms.
- Targeted cargo tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: expose probe and unplace lifecycle APIs
description:
Large gap: the TypeScript koffi binding still does not expose FFI lifecycle/introspection functions `kittui_probe_json` and `kittui_unplace`, even though shell/CLI and C ABI users can inspect runtime transport/ABI details and delete uploaded images. JS/TS hosts need the same renderer lifecycle controls.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_probe_json` and `kittui_unplace` in `bindings/ts/src/index.js`.
- Add `probe()` returning parsed JSON and `unplace(imageId)` returning delete bytes.
- Accept decimal numbers and hex strings for image ids if practical.
- Update TypeScript declarations and README docs.
- Add fake-library tests that assert probe parsing and unplace forwarding.

Acceptance:
- `Kittui.probe()` returns an object parsed from FFI JSON.
- `Kittui.unplace(0x1234)` forwards image id and returns delete bytes.
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Markdown tables via kittui box-drawing glyph atlas and relative placement anchors

Use kitty virtual placement plus relative non-virtual positioning anchors (P=/Q=/H=/V=) to position these cell images consistently, and support placing a background image or panel beneath the table in z-space. Include exact protocol/placement tests and visual proof fixtures.

title: kittwm: add explicit socket and display CLI flags
description:
Large DISPLAY-like usability gap: kittwm supports `KITTWM_SOCKET` / `KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY` env resolution, but CLI users must set environment variables to target a specific socket/display. First-class `--socket` and `--display` flags make the WM control plane easier and less error-prone for shell scripts and external controllers.

Scope:
- Add global CLI flags:
  - `--socket PATH` sets `KITTWM_SOCKET` / `KITTWM_SOCK` for this invocation.
  - `--display DISPLAY` sets `KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY` / `KITTWM_DISPLAY` for this invocation.
- Apply before any socket-resolving command (`--serve`, wrappers, save/restore, status, attach, etc.).
- Validate the flags are not both supplied.
- Update help/docs and add focused parse/normalization tests.

Acceptance:
- `--socket` and `--display` affect default socket resolution through existing daemon helper.
- Supplying both errors clearly.

title: kittwm: render and publish native PTY cursor visibility
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm tracks cursor position but the rendered PTY surface does not draw a cursor and does not honor DEC cursor visibility (`CSI ? 25 h/l`). Shells/editors rely on a visible caret; controllers can inspect cursor metadata but users cannot see it in the pane render.

Scope:
- Track cursor visibility in native `TerminalState` and handle DEC private `?25h` / `?25l`.
- Expose `PtyTerminalApp::cursor_visible()` and include optional `cursor_visible` in native pane status JSON/text surfaces.
- Render a visible cursor in `render_terminal_rgba` when enabled (without changing plain text snapshots).
- Update docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves cursor visibility mode toggles.
- Render test proves the cursor changes RGBA output at the cursor cell.
- Daemon status tests include cursor visibility metadata.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-python: expose runtime configure
description:
Large gap: FFI live runtime_configure is now real, but the Python binding still only configures at construction time. Long-lived Python hosts need to reconfigure renderer/transport/terminal capability without closing and reopening.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_runtime_configure` in Python ctypes binding.
- Add `Kittui.configure(config: dict)` returning None or self, using the same error handling/last_error path.
- Add fake-CDLL tests for success and failure.

Acceptance:

This lets 'kitwm --attach -c' exercise search+selection+launch against a running daemon. Parent: bd-031e54; extends bd-f92697/bd-68568a and decomposes bd-8d64b1.

title: kitty animation control grammar wrong: s={loops},c={count} misuses state/current-frame fields
description: Per kitty spec, on a=a (animation control) the s field is state (1=stop, 2=loop, 3=play N times) and c is the current frame to display; loop count goes in v. We emit s={loops},c={framecount} which sets state to a bogus value and forces current frame to last. Result: animations don't loop correctly and display wrong frame on start. Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md links-only mode for link inspection

description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes have visible title rows and socket status records, but there is no scriptable way to name/rename panes. Titles fall back to the spawned command, which is poor WM chrome for long-running shells and external controllers.

- Track an optional display title per native pane and use it in title chrome/status snapshots.
- Keep app command title as fallback when no display title is set.
- Add queue parsing and pane title helper tests.

title: kittwm: wait for text across screen and scrollback
description:
Gap after adding native scrollback: `WAIT_TEXT` only searches the current screen. Fast commands can print a sentinel and scroll it off-screen before controllers poll, so automation still misses output despite scrollback being available.

Scope:
- Add socket commands `WAIT_OUTPUT <window|focused> <needle>` and `WAIT_OUTPUT_MS <window|focused> <ms> <needle>` that search current screen plus native scrollback.
- Add CLI wrappers `--wait-output WINDOW TEXT` and `--wait-output-ms MS WINDOW TEXT`.
- Align client read timeout handling with the explicit millisecond form.
- Update help/docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `WAIT_OUTPUT` matches scrollback-only text while `WAIT_TEXT` remains screen-oriented.
- Targeted daemon/kittwm CLI tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support DEC Special Graphics line drawing
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not honor DEC Special Graphics character set selection (`ESC ( 0` / `ESC ( B`). Many curses apps still use ACS line-drawing bytes (`lqkx`, etc.) to draw borders. Native panes currently render those bytes literally, so boxes appear as `lqqk` / `x  x` in both the kitty-rendered frame and `READ_TEXT` snapshots.

Scope:
- Track G0 DEC Special Graphics selection in native `TerminalState`, default ASCII.
- Handle `ESC ( 0` to enable DEC Special Graphics and `ESC ( B` to return to ASCII.
- Map common DEC line-drawing glyphs (`l,m,k,j,q,x,t,u,v,w,n,plus/minus/dot variants`) to Unicode box-drawing/symbol characters during printable output.
- Reset charset selection on terminal reset; preserve it across resize like other modes.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test proves DEC ACS border bytes produce Unicode box-drawing glyphs.
- Test proves `ESC ( B` returns printable bytes to ASCII.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch: add standalone SDK app launcher skeleton
description: Add kittwm-launch as a standalone client that detects/accepts backend selection, spawns terminal/browser/GUI surfaces through SDK, and can replace current or create new windows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: expose render_many_png batch API
description:
Large gap: the Rust facade now has `render_many_png`, but FFI consumers still only have single-scene `kittui_render_json`. External platforms wanting batch preview artifacts need either many FFI round-trips or custom loops.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_render_many_json(runtime, scenes_json, out_json)` returning JSON manifest with count, per-image index/bytes/footprint/png_base64.
- Bump ABI minor and update `kittui.h`/ABI snapshot.
- Use `Runtime::render_many_png` internally.
- Add targeted FFI tests for PNG base64 manifest and non-array rejection.

Acceptance:
- `kittui_render_many_json` returns parseable JSON for scene arrays.
- Invalid non-array input returns BadScene with last_error.
- Targeted FFI and ABI snapshot tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support DEC origin mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap after scroll-region support: native kittwm does not honor DEC origin mode (`CSI ? 6 h/l`). TUIs that set scroll margins often enable origin mode so cursor addressing is relative to the scroll region; without it, writes land in the wrong rows and corrupt fixed headers/status lines.

Scope:
- Track DEC origin mode in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI ? 6 h` / `CSI ? 6 l`, resetting cursor to home on toggle.
- When origin mode is enabled, make `CSI H/f` row coordinates relative to `scroll_top` and clamped within `scroll_top..scroll_bottom`.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with scroll region + origin mode.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `CSI 1;1H` inside origin mode writes at scroll-region top, not absolute row 0.
- Tests show disabling origin mode restores absolute addressing.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add base64 byte injection command
description:
Large terminal automation gap: native kittwm socket input is line-oriented. `SEND_TEXT`/`SEND_LINE` cannot safely carry embedded newlines, NULs, escape sequences, or arbitrary paste payloads; scripts must work around protocol framing. A terminal WM control plane needs an encoded byte-injection path.

Scope:
- Add socket command `SEND_BYTES_B64 <window|focused> <base64>` that decodes bytes and sends them to the pane.
- Add CLI wrapper `kittwm --send-bytes-b64 <window|focused> <base64>`.
- Reuse existing `SendBytes` native loop handling.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON coverage, tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Base64 payloads decode to exact bytes and queue/send via `NativePaneCommand::SendBytes`.
- Invalid base64 and bad targets return clear errors.
- Targeted daemon/kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.

title: kittwm: render readable native terminal glyphs and reduce title flicker
description:
Native kittwm PTY panes currently render printable glyphs with synthetic pseudo-box strokes, making zsh/shell text unreadable despite interactivity. Replace the pseudo glyph renderer with a deterministic bitmap/vector glyph renderer for common terminal characters and reduce title-row flicker by avoiding unnecessary ANSI title redraws.

- Keep SGR foreground/background behavior and cursor rendering intact.
